source.title: Structural Damage - Crane/Other Lifting - Deepwater Asgard

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Green Canyon Block 895

event.top_event: Structural damage to marine riser and LMRP during hurricane evacuation
event.summary: On October 28, 2020, the Transocean Deepwater Asgard drillship was forced to evacuate location due to Hurricane Zeta. Despite initial forecasts indicating the storm would pass to the east, the hurricane's actual path brought it directly over the vessel. The decision to remain connected to the well until the last moment, combined with inaccurate weather forecasting, resulted in the drillship being pushed off location by extreme winds (peak gust 152 mph). During evacuation, the marine riser impacted the hull and the LMRP struck the seafloor twice, causing approximately $5.7 million in damage to the riser, LMRP, telescopic joint, mux cables, and subsea pods.
recommendations: BOE to recommend well securing, riser displacement, disconnection, and relocation to safe zone for storms projected to reach Category 1 Hurricane sustained winds or greater
Modify rig contract requirements to mandate riser, BOP, and tensioner system analysis for Category 1 Hurricane conditions between June 1 and December 1
BOE to compare projected versus actual weather conditions with weather service provider to eliminate conflicting information
Transocean to revise Extreme Weather Evacuation Plan (EWEP) to ensure compliance with operational limits
Transocean to request bathymetry maps to identify hazards in safe zone transit routes
BOE to modify Hurricane Evacuation Plan to include risk assessment and barrier evaluation considering time constraints
key_phrases: category 1 to category 2 hurricane force winds

hazards.name: Inadequate positioning control during severe weather

threats.name: Drillship drift off well location due to extreme winds
Marine riser impact with vessel hull
Decision to remain connected to well during approaching hurricane

consequences.name: Damage to Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP)
Damage to telescopic joint and subsea equipment

controls.name: Emergency Disconnect System (EDS)
controls.barrier_role: Rapid disconnection from wellhead to allow vessel evacuation
controls.lod_basis: Automated safety system for emergency well disconnection

supporting_text: At 09:40 hours the Asgard reached the red watch circle which was 159 feet off location. The EDS function was activated from the control panel at the Driller's chair, taking approximately 24 seconds to function.
the Emergency Disconnect System (EDS) was executed successfully

controls.name: Riser displacement with seawater
controls.barrier_role: Reduce pressure and secure well prior to disconnection
controls.lod_basis: Standard well securing procedure before hurricane evacuation

supporting_text: After the well was secured and the riser was displaced with seawater, the Emergency Disconnect System (EDS) was executed successfully.
The drill crew displaced the riser with seawater and pulled the RTTS running tool out of the hole to prepare to either disconnect from the wellhead or stay connected as the hurricane approached.

controls.name: Retrievable Test Treat Squeeze packer (RTTS) setting and pressure testing
controls.barrier_role: Isolate wellbore and verify well integrity before hurricane
controls.lod_basis: Well control and integrity verification procedure

supporting_text: The RTTS was set at 6,585 feet and a successful positive and negative test were accomplished.
The RTTS was set at 6,585 feet and was successfully pressure tested with 15.15 pounds per gallon (PPG) synthetic based mud (SBM), followed by a successful negative test.

controls.name: Weather monitoring and forecasting
controls.barrier_role: Provide advance warning of hurricane approach to enable timely evacuation decisions
controls.lod_basis: Early warning system for operational decision-making

supporting_text: Inaccurate weather forecast, the actual weather was worse than what was forecasted at the drillship's location. The original forecast said the storm would pass to the east of the Asgard's location and the storm would be a category 1 hurricane.
The Asgard experienced strong category 1 to category 2 hurricane force winds and extremely rough sea states, with a peak wind gust equal to a category 4 hurricane.
The documents obtained indicated that the projected storm path was to the East, therefore allowing the Asgard to remain connected to the well. As time progressed, the storm path began to change

controls.name: Thruster positioning system
controls.barrier_role: Maintain vessel position during severe weather to prevent drift off location

supporting_text: With these weather conditions, the Asgard was unable to maintain well center location with the thrusters at 100 percent output.
the drillship was being pushed in a Northwest direction while its thrusters were at 100 percent power in an effort to maintain well center.
The Asgard began to be pushed in a Northwest direction at a speed of 5.5 mph with thrusters still at 100 percent output.

controls.name: Air Pressure Vessel (APV) tensioner system
controls.barrier_role: Support weight of marine riser and LMRP after disconnection
controls.lod_basis: Subsea equipment support system during emergency conditions

supporting_text: At 170 feet off well center, the disconnect was complete and the subsea crew placed all Air Pressure Vessel (APV) bottles online which increased the tensioner pressure to support the weight of the marine riser and the LMRP.

controls.name: Slip joint locking mechanism
controls.barrier_role: Prevent slip joint movement and maintain riser stability during vessel drift
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical lock to stabilize riser during emergency conditions

supporting_text: by 10:12 hours, the slip joint was fully closed and had locked itself. The subsea team immediately applied the hydraulic locks to keep it locked in place.

controls.name: Iron Roughneck (IR) equipment maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Ensure drill string can be pulled from hole in timely manner before hurricane arrival
controls.lod_basis: Equipment reliability for well securing procedures

supporting_text: While POOH, the drill crew experienced mechanical problems with the Iron Roughneck (IR), which breaks the connections on the drill string. The IR remained out of service for most of Sunday, October 25th, 2020 until repairs could be made.
It was also noted in the documents that the IR had broken down multiple times due to mechanical issues, slowing the process of pulling the drill string out of the wellbore.

controls.name: Decision-making process for hurricane evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Ensure timely and appropriate decision to disconnect and evacuate before hurricane impact
controls.lod_basis: Operational decision-making protocol for extreme weather

supporting_text: A conference call between BOE and Transocean was held, and Transocean elected to stay connected to the well because previous sister drillships in similar conditions (Category 1 Hurricane Conditions) rode out storms without issues.
Human error, the decision was made to stay latched up to the well and ride out the storm.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
